title: The One with All the Poker

jokes.text: You're not the guy that asked for tea, are you?
jokes.setup: Monica lists an exhaustive catalog of tea varieties
jokes.punchline: Realizes she may have been overpreparing for someone who didn't even want tea
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica character moment - her obsessive hospitality meets practical reality. Great economy and timing.

jokes.text: Oh, good. Because where else would we get any?
jokes.setup: Rachel excited about free coffee sample
jokes.punchline: Monica sarcastically points out they work at a coffee shop
jokes.explanation: Solid sarcastic response but fairly predictable workplace humor

jokes.text: Lucky... ...to have had a friend like you.
jokes.setup: Monica seems to call Mindy 'lucky' (implying she's attractive)
jokes.punchline: Reveals she meant lucky to have had Rachel as a friend before stealing her fiancé
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection with perfect pause placement. Monica trying to be supportive while being accidentally cutting.

jokes.text: He's mastered the difference between 'bring me the' and 'pee in the.'
jokes.setup: Ross praising Marcel for bringing him rice
jokes.punchline: The alternative was the monkey peeing in things
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that establishes both Ross's pride in Marcel and the monkey's bathroom issues that become plot-relevant

jokes.text: The whole penis embargo?
jokes.setup: Rachel's previous declaration about being done with men
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's unexpected diplomatic term for celibacy
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly unexpected metaphor that's both sophisticated and silly. Pure Phoebe voice - treating serious topics with whimsical language.

jokes.text: ...gets interrupted.
jokes.setup: Ross building to romantic revelation about Rachel
jokes.punchline: Literally gets interrupted mid-sentence by the others returning
jokes.explanation: Meta-joke where the interruption IS the punchline. Good structure but relies on sitcom timing conventions.

jokes.text: ...and buses going really fast!
jokes.setup: Defending romantic movies against criticism
jokes.punchline: Reduces action movies to absurdly simple elements
jokes.explanation: Decent escalation structure but fairly predictable gendered movie criticism

jokes.text: Hugh! Hugh Grant!
jokes.setup: Joey's confusion about 'Lou Grant frolicking'
jokes.punchline: Chandler's exasperated correction
jokes.explanation: Simple correction joke playing on Joey's pop culture confusion. Functional but not special.

jokes.text: Unclench. You're not even gonna be here.
jokes.setup: Monica's uptight reaction to babysitting Marcel
jokes.punchline: Ross telling her to relax since she'll be out anyway
jokes.explanation: Perfect word choice for Monica's personality. 'Unclench' is both direct and character-specific.

jokes.text: I agree. I'm, like, in disbelief.
jokes.setup: Chandler saying he can't believe they're discussing this
jokes.punchline: Joey literally restating the same thing with different words
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey moment - thinks he's adding insight but just repeats the same thought. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: I added the 'exactly like me' part.
jokes.setup: Claiming Rachel wants someone exactly like him
jokes.punchline: Admitting he embellished her actual words
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Ross's self-awareness about his wishful thinking. Honest and endearing.

jokes.text: I can't remember the last time a girl took care of my monkey.
jokes.setup: Ross explaining his romantic plan
jokes.punchline: Innocent statement with obvious double entendre
jokes.explanation: Obvious double entendre but works because Ross is genuinely innocent about it. Character-appropriate innuendo.

jokes.text: Take her back to the 1890s when that phrase was last used.
jokes.setup: Ross saying he wants to 'woo' Rachel
jokes.punchline: Chandler mocking the outdated romantic language
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler snark with specific historical reference. Good setup/punchline structure.

jokes.text: Marcel, did you poo in the shoe?
jokes.setup: Rachel noticing Marcel playing with Monica's shoes
jokes.punchline: The horrible realization of what happened
jokes.explanation: Basic gross-out humor that sets up the main plot. Functional but relies on scatological comedy.

jokes.text: A little engagement gift. I'm sure you didn't register for that.
jokes.setup: Rachel disposing of the soiled shoe
jokes.punchline: Treating monkey poop as a wedding gift for her ex
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor that ties plot elements together. Rachel's petty revenge feels earned and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Those little clunky Amish things you think go with everything.
jokes.setup: Monica asking which shoes got ruined
jokes.punchline: Rachel's brutal but accurate description of Monica's fashion sense
jokes.explanation: Perfect character dynamic - Rachel's fashion expertise vs Monica's practical choices. 'Clunky Amish things' is hilariously specific.

jokes.text: Why is the air in here so negative?
jokes.setup: Everyone panicking about the lost monkey
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's New Age interpretation of the crisis energy
jokes.explanation: Pure Phoebe - treating practical crisis as spiritual energy problem. Perfect character voice.

jokes.text: I'll go to Cats. You go to the Russian Tea Room.
jokes.setup: Trying to think like a tourist monkey
jokes.punchline: Suggesting high-culture Manhattan destinations
jokes.explanation: Absurd logic treating a monkey like a sophisticated tourist. Good comedic misdirection.

jokes.text: Spray Lysol in my shoe and wait for Ross to kill you.
jokes.setup: Assigning tasks to find Marcel
jokes.punchline: Monica's practical approach to Rachel's impending doom
jokes.explanation: Monica's practical nature applied to dark humor. Good callback integration with efficient delivery.

jokes.text: I wasn't ready for it.
jokes.setup: Asked why he left a waffle in the hallway
jokes.punchline: His inexplicable relationship with breakfast foods
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd Mr. Heckles logic. The idea of not being 'ready' for a waffle is perfectly weird.

jokes.text: ...with Russian dressing and pickles on the side.
jokes.setup: Describing Marcel to building residents
jokes.punchline: Accidentally describing a sandwich instead of a monkey
jokes.explanation: Decent confusion comedy but relatively predictable mix-up structure.

jokes.text: ...the murder capital of the Northeast
jokes.setup: Rachel suggesting they go to Newark
jokes.punchline: Ross's casual description of Newark's reputation
jokes.explanation: Standard geographical stereotype humor. Functional but relies on easy target.

jokes.text: You should have started with a pencil.
jokes.setup: Ross saying Rachel shouldn't have started with a monkey
jokes.punchline: Suggesting an even more basic object to practice with
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke that shows Ross's frustration while being absurdly reductive about Rachel's capabilities.

jokes.text: We thought we had a monkey, but we didn't. -Turned out it was a hat. -Cat!
jokes.setup: Trying to cover up the monkey situation with Animal Control
jokes.punchline: Their terrible improvised lie keeps changing
jokes.explanation: Good panic comedy showing how bad lies spiral. The hat/cat correction is a nice touch.

jokes.text: You'd put that poor little creature in jail?
jokes.setup: Animal Control officer explaining legal consequences
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's naive misunderstanding of animal confiscation
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe innocence but fairly predictable character beat.

jokes.text: Remember how we talked about saying things quietly to yourself first?
jokes.setup: Phoebe blurting out incriminating information
jokes.punchline: Monica referencing an ongoing issue with Phoebe's impulse control
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency joke that implies a history of similar incidents.

jokes.text: Would it have been hard to say, 'Morning, Luisa' or 'Nice overalls'?
jokes.setup: Luisa revealing her high school resentment
jokes.punchline: Her specific memory of wanting basic acknowledgment, including fashion compliments
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing how small social slights can fester. The overalls detail adds specificity.

jokes.text: It's not so much you. You were fat. You had your own problems.
jokes.setup: Luisa explaining her high school resentments
jokes.punchline: Her brutal but honest assessment of Monica's situation
jokes.explanation: Harsh but surprisingly understanding analysis. Good subversion of expected mean girl behavior.

jokes.text: Of course! -Oh. Then, no.
jokes.setup: Asking if they can fix radiators, suggesting turning the knob
jokes.punchline: When that obvious solution was already tried, they have no expertise
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing their complete lack of practical skills once the obvious answer fails.

jokes.text: We do have expertise in the heating and cooling milieu.
jokes.setup: Trying to impress attractive women despite having no radiator skills
jokes.punchline: Using unnecessarily fancy language to describe basic HVAC knowledge
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler pretentiousness when trying to impress women. 'Milieu' is perfectly overwrought.

jokes.text: And they're very hot!
jokes.setup: Chandler saying the women are hot and need help
jokes.punchline: Joey repeating just the 'hot' part with different emphasis
jokes.explanation: Basic Joey horniness joke. Character-appropriate but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: From now on, you don't get to talk to other people.
jokes.setup: Joey's inappropriate behavior with the women
jokes.punchline: Chandler treating Joey like a misbehaving child
jokes.explanation: Good dynamic between Chandler's embarrassment and Joey's obliviousness. Parent/child structure works well.

jokes.text: Oh, it's okay. It was just my left leg.
jokes.setup: Phoebe feeling something brush against her leg
jokes.punchline: Relief that it was her own leg, not Marcel
jokes.explanation: Sweet Phoebe moment showing her spatial confusion. Simple but endearing.

jokes.text: You want me to break my foot too? I'll break it right now.
jokes.setup: Ross angry about Rachel losing Marcel and hurting her foot
jokes.punchline: Rachel's dramatic offer to injure herself more
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Rachel's guilt and drama. Shows her tendency toward grand gestures.

jokes.text: ...I don't miss Marcel anymore!
jokes.setup: Rachel's dramatic gesture of kicking the sign
jokes.punchline: Ross's sarcastic response that this doesn't help find Marcel
jokes.explanation: Solid sarcasm showing Ross's frustration. Good relationship dynamic but not exceptional.

jokes.text: You're off in 'Rachel Land,' doing your 'Rachel Thing'
jokes.setup: Ross's frustration with Rachel's behavior patterns
jokes.punchline: His specific terminology for her self-absorption
jokes.explanation: Great character-specific language that perfectly captures Ross's academic way of analyzing Rachel's personality flaws.

jokes.text: One side of my butt is totally asleep and the other side has no idea.
jokes.setup: Phoebe under sedation from tranquilizer dart
jokes.punchline: Her philosophical observation about partial numbness
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd Phoebe observation that treats body parts like separate entities. Perfect voice and quotable.

jokes.text: Potassium.
jokes.setup: Asked why he has bananas if he doesn't have a monkey
jokes.punchline: His matter-of-fact nutritional explanation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mr. Heckles deadpan. The scientific answer to suspicion about monkey ownership is beautifully absurd.

jokes.text: That's not my monkey. The dress is mine. Send it back whenever.
jokes.setup: Fight over monkey ownership between neighbors
jokes.punchline: Mr. Heckles' casual admission about cross-dressing
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection where the reveal isn't about the monkey but about his fashion choices. Perfect economy and timing.

jokes.text: You had to be a bitch in school, instead of fat.
jokes.setup: Luisa's final rejection of Rachel's appeal
jokes.punchline: Her bitter analysis of high school social hierarchies
jokes.explanation: Dark but honest social commentary. Shows how high school dynamics can provide adult motivation for petty revenge.

jokes.text: ...you shot my friend with a dart!
jokes.setup: Rachel threatening to call Luisa's supervisor
jokes.punchline: The completely legitimate complaint about Animal Control's aggressive tactics
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that makes the absurd situation sound reasonable when reported to authorities.

jokes.text: With the right pumps, that would be a great little outfit.
jokes.setup: Marcel wearing a dress from the neighbor
jokes.punchline: Ross's unexpectedly fashion-forward assessment
jokes.explanation: Unexpected fashion expertise from Ross. Good visual callback with practical fashion advice.

jokes.text: The neighbors must be vacuuming.
jokes.setup: Mysterious noise from upstairs during romantic moment
jokes.punchline: Ross's innocent explanation for what's obviously sexual activity
jokes.explanation: Classic sitcom innocence joke. Ross's naivety is character-appropriate but the setup is predictable.

jokes.text: We have got to start locking that door!
jokes.setup: Barry's dramatic interruption of romantic moment
jokes.punchline: Ross's practical response to repeated intrusions
jokes.explanation: Good practical punchline to romantic comedy interruption. Sets up potential running gag about apartment security.

jokes.text: I thought that was an alp.
jokes.setup: Monica showing childhood photo where she's blocking other kids
jokes.punchline: Chandler's geographical comparison to her size
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler snark with geographical precision. 'Alp' is a perfect specific reference for maximum impact.

jokes.text: Any sex I had would've involved a major lifestyle choice.
jokes.setup: Discussion about high school experiences at boarding school
jokes.punchline: Ross's matter-of-fact acknowledgment about same-sex environment
jokes.explanation: Good euphemistic humor that's both clever and character-appropriate for Ross's academic way of speaking.

jokes.text: My butt cheek is waking up!
jokes.setup: Earlier comment about her butt being partially asleep
jokes.punchline: Update on the recovery process with characteristic enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Good callback that maintains Phoebe's unique perspective on physical sensations. Nice episode bookend.

tmdb_overview: Rachel interviews for a Saks position; the women and men play an intensely competitive poker game.
